parts.title: SKILL I: BE SURE THE SENTENCE HAS A SUBJECT AND A VERB
parts.instruction: You know that a sentence in English should have a subject and a verb. The most common types of problems that you will encounter in structure questions on the TOEFL test have to do with subjects and verbs: perhaps the sentence is missing either the subject or the verb or both, or perhaps the sentence has an extra subject or verb.

questions.question: Last week went fishing for trout at the nearby mountain lake.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence lacks a subject. 'Last week' is an adverbial phrase of time, not a subject.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut tidak memiliki subjek. 'Last week' adalah frasa keterangan waktu, bukan subjek.

reason.en: This option is correct because the sentence is grammatically incomplete. It contains a verb ('went fishing') but lacks a subject (e.g., 'I' or 'He').
reason.id: Opsi ini benar karena kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa tidak lengkap. Kalimat ini mengandung kata kerja ('went fishing') tetapi tidak memiliki subjek (seperti 'I' atau 'He').

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: A schedule of the day's events can be obtained at the front desk.

reason.en: The sentence is grammatically correct. It contains a clear subject ('A schedule of the day's events') and a complete verb phrase ('can be obtained').
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa benar. Kalimat ini memiliki subjek yang jelas ('A schedule of the day's events') dan frasa kata kerja yang lengkap ('can be obtained').

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence already satisfies the requirement of having both a subject and a verb.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut sudah memenuhi syarat memiliki subjek dan kata kerja.

reason.en: Not applicable.

questions.question: A job on the day shift or the night shift at the plant available.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence is missing a main verb.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut tidak memiliki kata kerja utama.

reason.en: This option is correct. The sentence 'A job on the day shift or the night shift at the plant available' lacks a verb. To be grammatically correct, it should be 'A job... is available'.
reason.id: Opsi ini benar. Kalimat 'A job on the day shift or the night shift at the plant available' tidak memiliki kata kerja. Agar benar secara tata bahasa, seharusnya menjadi 'A job... is available'.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: The new computer program has provides a variety of helpful applications.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence contains a grammatical error in the verb phrase.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut mengandung kesalahan tata bahasa pada frasa kata kerja.

reason.en: The sentence is incorrect because the verb phrase 'has provides' is grammatically invalid. After the auxiliary verb 'has', the past participle form 'provided' must be used, not the base form 'provides'.
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut salah karena frasa kata kerja 'has provides' tidak valid secara tata bahasa. Setelah kata kerja bantu 'has', bentuk past participle 'provided' harus digunakan, bukan bentuk dasar 'provides'.

reason.en: Not applicable.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: The box can be opened only with a special screwdriver.

reason.en: [please evaluate-manual] This option is marked as incorrect by the key, but the sentence 'The box can be opened only with a special screwdriver' is grammatically correct. It contains a subject ('The box') and a complete passive verb phrase ('can be opened').
reason.id: [please evaluate-manual] Opsi ini ditandai salah oleh kunci jawaban, namun kalimat 'The box can be opened only with a special screwdriver' secara tata bahasa sudah benar. Kalimat ini memiliki subjek ('The box') dan frasa kata kerja pasif yang lengkap ('can be opened').

reason.en: The key indicates this is incorrect, likely due to a mismatch in the provided answer key logic, as the sentence is actually a complete and grammatically sound structure.
reason.id: Kunci jawaban menunjukkan ini salah, kemungkinan karena ketidaksesuaian logika kunci jawaban yang diberikan, karena kalimat tersebut sebenarnya adalah struktur yang lengkap dan benar secara tata bahasa.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: The assigned text for history class it contains more than twenty chapters.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence is grammatically flawed due to a double subject.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut cacat secara tata bahasa akibat penggunaan subjek ganda.

reason.en: This option is correct. The sentence contains a double subject: 'The assigned text' and 'it'. According to Skill 1, a sentence must have one subject and one verb; having both 'The assigned text' and 'it' as subjects makes the sentence grammatically incorrect.
reason.id: Opsi ini benar. Kalimat tersebut mengandung subjek ganda: 'The assigned text' dan 'it'. Berdasarkan Skill 1, sebuah kalimat harus memiliki satu subjek dan satu kata kerja; penggunaan 'The assigned text' dan 'it' secara bersamaan sebagai subjek membuat kalimat tersebut salah secara tata bahasa.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: The papers in the wastebasket should be emptied into the trash can outside.

reason.en: The sentence is correct because it contains a clear subject ('The papers') and a complete verb phrase ('should be emptied'). The phrase 'in the wastebasket' is a prepositional phrase modifying the subject, and 'into the trash can outside' is a prepositional phrase indicating location.
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut benar karena memiliki subjek yang jelas ('The papers') dan frasa kata kerja yang lengkap ('should be emptied'). Frasa 'in the wastebasket' adalah frasa preposisional yang menjelaskan subjek, dan 'into the trash can outside' adalah frasa preposisional yang menunjukkan keterangan tempat.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence already possesses a valid subject and verb structure, making it grammatically sound.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut sudah memiliki struktur subjek dan kata kerja yang valid, sehingga secara tata bahasa sudah tepat.

reason.en: Not applicable.

reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut tidak memiliki subjek. 'Yesterday' adalah keterangan waktu, bukan subjek.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: The new machine is processes 50 percent more than the previous machine.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence contains a grammatical error in the verb phrase.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut mengandung kesalahan tata bahasa pada frasa kata kerja.

reason.en: The sentence is incorrect because it uses a double verb structure 'is processes'. The verb should either be 'is processing' (present continuous) or simply 'processes' (simple present).
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut salah karena menggunakan struktur kata kerja ganda 'is processes'. Kata kerjanya seharusnya 'is processing' (present continuous) atau cukup 'processes' (simple present).

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

parts.title: SKILL 2: BE CAREFUL OF OBJECTS OF PREPOSITIONS
parts.instruction: An object of a preposition is a noun, pronoun, gerund or noun clause that comes after a preposition, such as in, at, of, to, by, behind, on, and so on, to form a prepositional phrase.

questions.question: The interviews by radio broadcasters were carried live by the station.

reason.en: The sentence is grammatically correct. The subject is 'The interviews' (plural), and the verb is 'were carried' (passive voice, plural). 'by radio broadcasters' is a prepositional phrase, and 'by the station' is another prepositional phrase; neither contains the subject.
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa benar. Subjeknya adalah 'The interviews' (jamak), dan kata kerjanya adalah 'were carried' (kalimat pasif, jamak). 'by radio broadcasters' adalah frasa preposisional, dan 'by the station' adalah frasa preposisional lainnya; tidak ada satu pun yang mengandung subjek.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence structure already satisfies the requirement of having a subject ('The interviews') and a verb ('were carried').
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena struktur kalimat sudah memenuhi syarat memiliki subjek ('The interviews') dan kata kerja ('were carried').

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 2: Be Careful of Objects of Prepositions

questions.question: in the last possible moment before takeoff took his seat in the airplane.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence lacks a subject. The phrase 'in the last possible moment' is a prepositional phrase, and 'before takeoff' is another prepositional phrase; neither can function as the subject of the sentence.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut tidak memiliki subjek. Frasa 'in the last possible moment' adalah frasa preposisi, dan 'before takeoff' juga merupakan frasa preposisi; keduanya tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai subjek kalimat.

reason.en: This option is correct because the sentence is grammatically incomplete. It contains prepositional phrases ('in the last possible moment', 'before takeoff') and a verb ('took'), but it is missing a subject (e.g., 'The passenger') to perform the action.
reason.id: Opsi ini benar karena kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa tidak lengkap. Kalimat ini mengandung frasa preposisi ('in the last possible moment', 'before takeoff') dan kata kerja ('took'), tetapi kehilangan subjek (misalnya, 'The passenger') untuk melakukan tindakan tersebut.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 2: Be Careful of Objects of Prepositions

questions.question: At the neighborhood flower shop, flowers in quantities of a dozen or a half dozen can be delivered for free.

reason.en: The sentence is grammatically correct. The subject is 'flowers', and the verb is 'can be delivered'. The phrases 'At the neighborhood flower shop' and 'in quantities of a dozen or a half dozen' are prepositional phrases that do not contain the subject.
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa sudah benar. Subjeknya adalah 'flowers', dan kata kerjanya adalah 'can be delivered'. Frasa 'At the neighborhood flower shop' dan 'in quantities of a dozen or a half dozen' adalah frasa preposisional yang tidak mengandung subjek.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence structure follows the rules of English grammar, correctly identifying the subject and verb while properly using prepositional phrases.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena struktur kalimat tersebut mengikuti aturan tata bahasa Inggris, dengan mengidentifikasi subjek dan kata kerja secara tepat serta menggunakan frasa preposisional dengan benar.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 2: Be Careful of Objects of Prepositions

questions.question: The progressive reading methods at this school are given credit for the improved test scores.

reason.en: The sentence is grammatically correct. The subject is 'methods' (plural), and the verb is 'are given' (plural). The phrase 'at this school' is a prepositional phrase, and 'at this school' does not contain the subject.
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa sudah benar. Subjeknya adalah 'methods' (jamak), dan kata kerjanya adalah 'are given' (jamak). Frasa 'at this school' adalah frasa preposisi, dan 'at this school' tidak mengandung subjek.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence structure follows the rules of subject-verb agreement and correctly identifies the subject 'methods' separate from the prepositional phrase.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena struktur kalimat mengikuti aturan kesesuaian subjek-kata kerja dan mengidentifikasi subjek 'methods' secara benar terpisah dari frasa preposisi.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

questions.question: For the last three years at various hospitals in the county has been practicing medicine.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence lacks a subject. 'For the last three years' and 'at various hospitals in the county' are both prepositional phrases, which cannot function as the subject.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut tidak memiliki subjek. 'For the last three years' dan 'at various hospitals in the county' keduanya adalah frasa preposisional, yang tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai subjek.

reason.en: This option is correct because the sentence is grammatically incomplete. It contains prepositional phrases but lacks a subject to perform the action 'has been practicing'.
reason.id: Opsi ini benar karena kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa tidak lengkap. Kalimat ini mengandung frasa preposisional tetapi tidak memiliki subjek untuk melakukan tindakan 'has been practicing'.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

reason.en: Not applicable.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 2: Be Careful of Objects of Prepositions

questions.question: In the past a career in politics was not considered acceptable in some circles.

reason.en: The sentence is grammatically correct. It contains a clear subject ('a career') and a verb ('was not considered'). The phrase 'in politics' is a prepositional phrase acting as an adjective, and 'in some circles' is a prepositional phrase acting as an adverbial of place; neither contains the subject.
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa sudah benar. Kalimat ini memiliki subjek yang jelas ('a career') dan kata kerja ('was not considered'). Frasa 'in politics' adalah frasa preposisional yang berfungsi sebagai kata sifat, dan 'in some circles' adalah frasa preposisional yang berfungsi sebagai keterangan tempat; tidak ada satu pun yang mengandung subjek.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence structure follows standard English grammar rules, including proper subject-verb agreement and correct placement of prepositional phrases.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena struktur kalimat tersebut mengikuti aturan tata bahasa Inggris standar, termasuk kesesuaian subjek-kata kerja yang tepat dan penempatan frasa preposisional yang benar.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

questions.question: Shopping in the downtown area of the city it has improved a lot in recent years.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence contains a grammatical error.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut mengandung kesalahan tata bahasa.

reason.en: The sentence is incorrect because it contains a double subject. 'Shopping' is the subject, but the pronoun 'it' is redundant and unnecessary. Additionally, 'in the downtown area of the city' is a prepositional phrase that does not contain the subject.
reason.id: Kalimat ini salah karena mengandung subjek ganda. 'Shopping' adalah subjeknya, sehingga kata ganti 'it' menjadi redundan dan tidak diperlukan. Selain itu, 'in the downtown area of the city' adalah frasa preposisi yang tidak mengandung subjek.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 2: Be Careful of Objects of Prepositions

questions.question: At the building site the carpenters with the most experience were given the most intricate work.

reason.en: The sentence is grammatically correct. The subject is 'carpenters' and the verb is 'were given'. The phrase 'at the building site' is a prepositional phrase, and 'with the most experience' is another prepositional phrase modifying 'carpenters'. Neither contains the subject.
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa benar. Subjeknya adalah 'carpenters' dan kata kerjanya adalah 'were given'. Frasa 'at the building site' adalah frasa preposisional, dan 'with the most experience' adalah frasa preposisional lain yang menjelaskan 'carpenters'. Tidak ada satu pun dari frasa tersebut yang mengandung subjek.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence structure follows the rules of English grammar, containing a clear subject and verb.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena struktur kalimat tersebut mengikuti aturan tata bahasa Inggris, dengan memiliki subjek dan kata kerja yang jelas.

reason.en: The sentence is correct because it contains a clear subject ('The report') and a verb ('was delivered'). The phrase 'with complete documentation' is a prepositional phrase acting as an modifier, and 'at the conference' is another prepositional phrase indicating location; neither contains the subject.

Frasa 'with complete documentation' adalah frasa preposisional yang berfungsi sebagai penjelas, dan 'at the conference' adalah frasa preposisional lain yang menunjukkan lokasi; keduanya tidak mengandung subjek.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 2: Be Careful of Objects of Prepositions

parts.title: SKILL 3: BE CAREFUL OF APPOSITIVES
parts.instruction: An appositive is a noun that comes before or after another noun and has the same meaning.

questions.question: The son of the previous owneiy the new owner is undertaking some fairly broad changes in management policy.

reason.en: The sentence is grammatically correct. 'The son of the previous owner' acts as an appositive phrase renaming the subject 'the new owner', and the sentence contains a clear subject ('the new owner') and a verb ('is undertaking').
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa benar. 'The son of the previous owner' berfungsi sebagai frasa apositif yang menjelaskan subjek 'the new owner', dan kalimat tersebut memiliki subjek ('the new owner') serta kata kerja ('is undertaking') yang jelas.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence structure follows the rules for appositives correctly, making the 'INCORRECT' label invalid.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena struktur kalimat mengikuti aturan apositif dengan benar, sehingga label 'INCORRECT' tidak valid.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 3: Be Careful of Appositives

questions.question: Last semester, a friend, graduated cum laude from the university.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence is grammatically flawed.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut memiliki cacat tata bahasa.

reason.en: The sentence is incorrect because it lacks a subject. 'A friend' is an appositive renaming a missing subject, and 'graduated' is the verb. The structure requires a subject before the appositive (e.g., 'My friend, a classmate, graduated...').
reason.id: Kalimat ini salah karena tidak memiliki subjek. 'A friend' adalah aposisi yang menjelaskan subjek yang hilang, dan 'graduated' adalah kata kerjanya. Struktur ini memerlukan subjek sebelum aposisi (contoh: 'My friend, a classmate, graduated...').

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak tersedia.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak tersedia.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 3: Be Careful of Appositives

questions.question: Valentine's Day, February 14, is a special holiday for sweethearts.

reason.en: The sentence is grammatically correct. 'Valentine's Day' is the subject, 'February 14' is the appositive renaming the subject, and 'is' is the main verb.
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa sudah benar. 'Valentine's Day' adalah subjek, 'February 14' adalah aposisi yang menjelaskan kembali subjek, dan 'is' adalah kata kerja utama.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence structure follows the rules for appositives correctly, meaning it is not an error.
reason.id: Pilihan ini salah karena struktur kalimat tersebut mengikuti aturan aposisi dengan benar, sehingga tidak terdapat kesalahan.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak tersedia.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak tersedia.

questions.question: At long last, the chief executive officer, has decided to step down.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence contains a grammatical error regarding the use of commas with the appositive.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut mengandung kesalahan tata bahasa terkait penggunaan koma dengan aposisi.

reason.en: This option is correct. The sentence is grammatically incorrect because the appositive 'the chief executive officer' is set off by only one comma instead of two, or it is incorrectly placed, creating a separation between the subject and the verb that is not properly punctuated.
reason.id: Opsi ini benar. Kalimat tersebut secara tata bahasa salah karena aposisi 'the chief executive officer' hanya dipisahkan oleh satu koma, bukan dua, atau penempatannya salah sehingga menciptakan pemisahan antara subjek dan kata kerja yang tidak diberi tanda baca dengan benar.

reason.en: Not applicable.
reason.id: Tidak berlaku.

reason.en: Not applicable.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 3: Be Careful of Appositives

questions.question: Tonight's supper, leftovers from last night, did not taste any better tonight than last night.

reason.en: The sentence is grammatically correct. 'Tonight's supper' is the subject, 'leftovers from last night' is the appositive renaming the subject, and 'did not taste' is the verb.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 3: Be Careful of Appositives

parts.title: SKILL 4: BE CAREFUL OF PRESENT PARTICIPLES
parts.instruction:
A present participle is the -ing form of the verb (talking, playing).
it can be either a part of the verb or an adjective. 

It is part of the verb when it is preceded by some form of the verb be.
it is an adjective when it is not accompanied by some form of the verb be.

questions.question: The companies offering the lowest prices will have the most customers.

reason.en: The sentence is correct. 'Offering' is a present participle acting as an adjective modifying 'companies', and 'will have' is the main verb of the sentence.
reason.id: Kalimat tersebut benar. 'Offering' adalah present participle yang berfungsi sebagai kata sifat untuk menerangkan 'companies', dan 'will have' adalah kata kerja utama dalam kalimat tersebut.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence structure is grammatically sound and follows the rules for present participles used as adjectives.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena struktur kalimatnya sudah tepat secara tata bahasa dan mengikuti aturan penggunaan present participle sebagai kata sifat.

reason.en: This option is not applicable.

Seharusnya menjadi 'Those travelers completing their trip on Delta should report...' (di mana 'should report' adalah kata kerja utama) atau 'Those travelers are completing their trip on Delta' (di mana 'are completing' adalah kata kerja utama).

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - SKILL 4: BE CAREFUL OF PRESENT PARTICIPLES

parts.title: SKILL 5: BE CAREFUL OF PAST PARTICIPLES
parts.instruction:
The past participle is the form of the verb that appears with have or be. It often ends in -ed, but there are also many irregular past participles in English

The family ~[has purchased]~ a television. 
it part of the verb because it is accompanied by has.

The television ~[purchased]~  yesterday was expensive.
A past participle is an adjective when it is not accompanied by some form of be or have.

questions.question: The money was offered by the client was not accepted

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence is grammatically flawed.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kalimat tersebut memiliki kesalahan tata bahasa.

reason.en: The sentence is incorrect because it contains two main verbs ('was offered' and 'was not accepted') without a connector. To be correct, 'was offered' should be reduced to a past participle phrase ('The money offered by the client was not accepted') or joined by a relative pronoun ('The money that was offered by the client was not accepted').
reason.id: Kalimat ini salah karena mengandung dua kata kerja utama ('was offered' dan 'was not accepted') tanpa kata penghubung. Agar benar, 'was offered' seharusnya direduksi menjadi frasa past participle ('The money offered by the client was not accepted') atau dihubungkan dengan kata ganti relatif ('The money that was offered by the client was not accepted').

It should be 'The courses listed on the second page of the brochure have several prerequisites' (if 'listed' is an adjective) or 'The courses are listed...' (if 'are listed' is the passive verb). As written, 'are listed' creates a double verb conflict.

'Listed' di sini adalah past participle yang berfungsi sebagai kata sifat, namun kalimat ini kekurangan kata kerja utama. Seharusnya 'The courses listed on the second page of the brochure have several prerequisites' (jika 'listed' sebagai kata sifat) atau 'The courses are listed...' (jika 'are listed' sebagai kata kerja pasif). Dalam bentuk yang tertulis, 'are listed' menciptakan konflik kata kerja ganda.
